repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §1 Q1 panel FWL/sign-flip catalogue is discrete-regression contamination, not continuous ACR support-gap bounds

published_prior_art.cite: Manski2003 and KimKwonKwonLee2018 provide generic set-ID/smoothness-bound templates; not the DID derivative/intercept target

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1 has DID support-function intervals for bounded anticipation; pid_dose_response_iv_v1 has finite LP slope bounds under IV/rank copula; neither is no-stayer continuous-DID C^{1,1} support gaps

published_prior_art.cite: DCDHK2026NoUntreated/DCDHVB2024NoStayers motivate no-stayer/no-quasi cases; LeGruyer2009/Wells1973-type C^{1,1} extension theory is a closer mathematical comparator than Whitney/McShane alone
DCDHPSVB2025ContinuousEveryPeriod identifies local switcher slopes with same-baseline stayers; DCDHK2026NoUntreated gives the quasi-untreated local-slope benchmark

structure_flags.label: Locked parameters
structure_flags.one_line: The qid is prefixed eid_ while the locked tuple, theorem statements, and scope are PartialID support-bound objects; reroute/pin the proposal as a pid_ partial-identification anchor or explicitly justify the ExactID container.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey misses the closest C^{1,1} extension/minimal Lipschitz-gradient literature such as Wells 1973 and Le Gruyer 2009; tier=field below novelty_target=flagship unless the DID intercept/support-gap contribution is shown to be a strict new open-regime result.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The observed-support local-slope collapse is already the point-ID corner of the continuous-DID stayer/quasi-untreated literature, so it should remain only a sanity check.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The clipped-arc blocks use endpoint values and one-sided derivative cones at support boundaries, but §6/§7 only state C^1 knowledge on interiors and do not pin endpoint trace conventions for m_P.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The claim that every feasible p is attained on an open neighborhood of P is not well-defined until a topology/metric on observable laws and support perturbations is specified.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Retitle/reroute the proposal as a PartialID qid or add a precise explanation for why an eid_ path is hosting a sharp interval target.

Add Wells 1973, Le Gruyer 2009, and one modern C^{1,1}/Glaeser-Whitney extension comparator, then state exactly what the scalar DID intercept and observed-support topology add beyond classical extension theory.
Define endpoint traces, one-sided observed derivatives, derivative cones, and the topology for the open-neighborhood sharpness clause before stating the finite program.

To meet the flagship floor, tie the conjecture to a named open gap in DCDHK2026NoUntreated or DCDHVB2024NoStayers and prove a strict nonparametric improvement over their no-quasi/no-stayer branch on an open class of observable laws.

recommended_next_step: Revise locally: fix the PartialID routing, patch endpoint/topology conventions, add the missing C^{1,1} prior art, and sharpen the claim to a flagship-level open-regime result.
